title: Cephaelis hastisepala
intro: Cephaelis hastisepala, also known as Ipecacuanha or Brazilian Ipecac, is a plant species native to South America that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Cephaelis hastisepala include its ability to induce vomiting and treat various gastrointestinal disorders, as well as its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to alleviate symptoms of asthma, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis hastisepala include alkaloids such as emetine and cephaeline, which are responsible for its emetic properties and potential therapeutic applications. Traditionally, Cephaelis hastisepala is used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and syrups, often in combination with other herbs, to treat a range of health conditions, including nausea, diarrhea, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Cephaelis hastisepala is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cephaelis, which contains over 100 species of plants. The plant is native to Central and South America, where it grows in tropical and subtropical regions.
